Convert the tax liability for the art collection by applying the 20% tax rate to the $2 million appraised value, then applying the 5% discount on the taxable amount. Convert the taxable amount of the art collection to $400,000 before applying the 5% discount, resulting in a tax liability of $380,000.

Convert the real estate portfolio tax liability by first calculating the tax for each bracket. Convert the first $1 million to a 10% tax rate, resulting in $100,000. Convert the next $2 million to a 15% tax rate, resulting in $300,000. Convert the remaining $2 million (after the 10% underestimation) to a 20% tax rate, resulting in $400,000. Convert the total real estate tax liability to $800,000.

Convert the securities portfolio tax liability by applying the 15% tax rate to the $3 million value, resulting in $450,000. Convert the $100,000 deduction to reduce the tax liability to $350,000.

Convert the total tax liability for the estate by summing the liabilities from each asset. Convert the art collection tax liability of $380,000, the real estate tax liability of $800,000, and the securities tax liability of $350,000. Convert the total tax liability to $1,530,000.

Convert the real estate portfolio value by adjusting for the 10% underestimation. Convert the original $5 million value to $5.5 million. Convert the tax calculation for the real estate portfolio by recalculating each bracket with the new value. Convert the first $1 million to a 10% tax rate, resulting in $100,000. Convert the next $2 million to a 15% tax rate, resulting in $300,000. Convert the remaining $2.5 million to a 20% tax rate, resulting in $500,000. Convert the total real estate tax liability to $900,000.
